title: Using expressions in attributes results in dropped attributes
body:
## Steps to reproduce the issue

Use yosys to read this verilog file :

```verilog
module bug (
    input wire iw,
    output wire ow
);

    localparam j = 2; 

    (* A=2 *)
    (* B=j+1 *)
    SB_LUT4 #(
        .LUT_INIT(16'h0001)
    ) test_I (
        .I0(iw),
        .I1(),  
        .I2(),  
        .I3(),  
        .O(ow)  
    );

endmodule
```

## Expected behavior

In the resulting .json or .blif, the cell should have two attributes `A` & `B`.

## Actual behavior

But in reality, only `B` will end up being present.

And dumping the AST tree using `read_verilog -dump_ast1 bug.v`, you can see it too : 

```
      AST_CELL <bug.v:11> [0xea98f0] str='\test_I'
        ATTR \B:
          AST_ADD <bug.v:10> [0xea9d70]
            AST_IDENTIFIER <bug.v:10> [0xea9ea0] str='\j'
            AST_CONSTANT <bug.v:10> [0xea9fb0] bits='00000000000000000000000000000001'(32) signed range=[31:0] int=1
        AST_CELLTYPE <bug.v:11> [0xea9a00] str='\SB_LUT4'
        AST_PARASET <bug.v:12> [0xea9b10] str='\LUT_INIT'
          AST_CONSTANT <bug.v:12> [0xea9c40] bits='0000000000000001'(16) range=[15:0] int=1
        AST_ARGUMENT <bug.v:14> [0xeaa1d0] str='\I0'
          AST_IDENTIFIER <bug.v:14> [0xeaa0c0] str='\iw'
        AST_ARGUMENT <bug.v:15> [0xeaa2e0] str='\I1'
        AST_ARGUMENT <bug.v:16> [0xeaa3f0] str='\I2'
        AST_ARGUMENT <bug.v:17> [0xeaa500] str='\I3'
        AST_ARGUMENT <bug.v:18> [0xeaa720] str='\O'
          AST_IDENTIFIER <bug.v:18> [0xeaa610] str='\ow'
```

Only `B` is present.
